title: At the Four Seasons in Damascus, power shifts but the elite remain
subhead: A symbol of Bashar al-Assad's grip on Syria, this luxury hotel, which opened in 2005 in the heart of the capital, was the scene of lavish parties and business dealings among those close to the regime, until the fall of the dictator in December 2024. For a time, it served as the headquarters of the new leaders; today, the establishment is a networking hub for entrepreneurs seeking business opportunities in a country where everything must be rebuilt.

'Their mouths, eyes, hands, were taped and they were thrown into the hole': Russian soldiers confront Putin with reality of front lines
subhead: A Russian exile media outlet has analyzed nearly 7,000 letters from Russian soldiers and their families to the Kremlin thanks to a security flaw.

title: 250 years of American independence: The retreat from multiracial democracy
subhead: 'America 250' (7/13). A century after its founding, 38 states made up the nation. Deep divisions persisted over how to treat the Black population. Slavery had been abolished, much to the dismay of the Democrats, who were gaining ground. Although the Republicans won the presidential election, their victory came at the cost of institutionalizing segregationist policies in the Southern states.

title: 2026 World Cup: Canada advances to the round of 16 after last-minute victory over South Africa
subhead: Buoyed by Stephen Eustaquio’s goal in stoppage time (1-0), the Canadians have secured the first spot in the next elimination round and are set to face either the Netherlands or Morocco.

title: France's fantasy balls: 'We identify with the lives of castle dwellers from a bygone era'
subhead: Blending historical and fantastical references, fantasy balls promise a leap through time and a touch of magic in worlds reminiscent of the 'Bridgerton' series or 'Alice in Wonderland.' These evenings are as much about dance as they are about spectacle.

subhead: The launch of a heritage pass, the search for sponsors and the commitment of volunteers all point to the same effort: those working to preserve France's heritage sites are looking for new ways to avoid a looming funding gap.

title: From a Russian prison to a European music festival, the escape of a young anti-Putin singer
subhead: On June 12, artist Diana Loginova performed among the stars of Russia's dissident music scene at the Outloud festival in Warsaw. She took to the stage after managing to escape from Russia. In 2025, she was in prison for singing songs condemned by the Kremlin.

title: Mansplashing: Why do men splash everyone at the pool?
subhead: The behavior of the so-called 'pool man,' who thrashes around in the water and sprawls out across swimming lanes, highlights the power dynamics at play in the shared social space of the public swimming pool.
